before.task: Preserve legacy handoff-history config and launchd compatibility across btrain rename
before.nextAction: Approved. Legacy migration logic is correct: install script falls back to old config, migrates watch-list entries, boots out the old label. Register script dual-writes to both watch lists and restarts both agents. All 85 tests pass including 2 new regression tests. Only gap is manual launchctl smoke test on a real machine, which is acceptable given the fake-launchctl coverage.

after.nextAction: Treat the first btrain poll as baseline (no notifications), and make cleanup retain the last 200 messages per channel only after a channel actually exceeds 200. Add regression coverage for both paths before re-handoff.

details.summary: Found two blocking regressions: the first btrain snapshot now posts duplicate handoff notifications on every server restart, and the new cleanup loop starts trimming at 101 messages but only preserves 100 while logging negative trim counts.

details.summary: The poller fixes in app.py look right, but lane f still leaves wrapper verification red: agentchattr/tests/test_btrain_context.py fails because wrapper.py changed the injected lane wording without updating the existing assertions. The new poller regression coverage also exists only as an untracked file, so it is not actually part of the handoff yet.
